audit_items.proof_trace: AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() returns NULL on allocation failure (random-private.h:66-67, 72-73), but SpreadImage() performs no NULL check before dereferencing random_info[0] at effect.c:3804 (OpenMP path) and random_info[id] at effect.c:3833 (pixel loop). Additionally, DestroyRandomInfoThreadSet() at effect.c:3857 asserts random_info != NULL, so any path through the function crashes if the allocation fails.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker submits an image processed with the -spread effect under controlled memory pressure (e.g., resource limits or concurrent allocations); AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() returns NULL, and the immediate dereference of random_info[0] or random_info[id] causes a NULL pointer dereference crash (DoS).
